name: Nicatoridae
description: Nicators are a small family of passerine birds found in sub-Saharan Africa. They are bulky, thrush-like birds with heavy hooked bills adapted for catching large insects and small vertebrates.

children.name: Nicator
children.description: A genus of thrush-sized passerines with stout bills and olive or grey plumage, found across African woodlands.

children.name: Nicator chloris
children.description: The Western Nicator is an olive-green bird with yellow underparts and a loud, melodious song. It inhabits lowland rainforest across west and central Africa.

children.name: Nicator gularis
children.description: The Eastern Nicator has olive upperparts and pale underparts with a distinctive white throat. It is found in coastal and riverine thickets from Somalia to South Africa.

children.name: Nicator vireo
children.description: The Yellow-spotted Nicator is the smallest of the nicators, with yellow spots on its wing coverts. It inhabits lowland forests of central Africa and feeds on insects in the canopy.
